name: Klocwork Static Code Analysis API
what_it_does: Static code analysis platform specialized for C/C++, Java, and C# in embedded and safety-critical systems — enabling engineering teams to find security vulnerabilities, memory errors, concurrency issues, and MISRA/AUTOSAR/CWE compliance violations, with a REST API for CI/CD integration, a server-based architecture (Klocwork Server) for team result management, and strong support for automotive, aerospace, and medical device software standards.
use_cases: Scanning embedded C/C++ firmware for memory safety and concurrency bugs via Klocwork in CI/CD
Enforcing MISRA C/C++ compliance via Klocwork for automotive software (ISO 26262, AUTOSAR)
Finding security vulnerabilities in safety-critical software via Klocwork for aerospace (DO-178C)
Managing static analysis results via Klocwork Server API for enterprise AppSec programs
Generating compliance reports via Klocwork API for medical device certification (IEC 62443)
not_for: Web application security (use Semgrep or Checkmarx for web app SAST)
Open-source dependency scanning (use Snyk or Black Duck for SCA)
Teams without C/C++ or Java embedded systems focus
best_when: An embedded systems or safety-critical software team needs deep C/C++ SAST with MISRA/AUTOSAR compliance checking for automotive, aerospace, or medical device certification.
avoid_when: You need web app SAST, SCA, or general-purpose enterprise SAST — Klocwork specializes in embedded and safety-critical C/C++.

auth.notes: Klocwork access token (ltoken) from Klocwork Server. Token stored in ~/.klocwork/ltoken file. API requests: user parameter + ltoken parameter in URL. REST API at https://{klocwork-server}:{port}/review/api. On-premise deployment. Role-based access control via Klocwork Server user management. No OAuth2. No webhooks. JSON responses. kwauth CLI tool for token management.

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise: custom pricing (Perforce quote); significant cost for safety-critical toolchain licensing
pricing.notes: Originally Klocwork Inc. (Ottawa). Founded 2000. Acquired by Perforce Software 2019. Perforce also owns Helix Core SCM and other DevOps tools. C/C++ focus: buffer overflow, null pointer dereference, memory leaks, race conditions, integer overflow. MISRA C 2004/2012, MISRA C++ 2008 checker. AUTOSAR C++14 guidelines. CWE/CVE mapping. DO-178C (avionics). ISO 26262 (automotive ASIL A-D). IEC 62443 (industrial). Medical device: IEC 62304. IDE integration: Eclipse, VS, IntelliJ. Server-client architecture: kwcheck (local scan) → Klocwork Server (team view). Desktop analysis mode (no server required for individual dev use). Heavily used in automotive OEMs and Tier 1 suppliers.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: ltoken file-based auth. On-premise Klocwork Server URL required. Perforce ownership. MISRA/AUTOSAR focus. Safety-critical standards. Enterprise pricing. No OAuth2. No webhooks.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Issue reads idempotent by issue ID. Build reads idempotent. Status update operations idempotent by issue ID.
known_agent_gotchas: Auth uses ltoken file (~/.klocwork/ltoken) — run kwauth first to create token
Self-hosted: base URL is your Klocwork Server (e.g., https://klocwork.company.com:8080/review/api)
Scan workflow: kwbuildproject (build capture) → kwcheck (local analysis) → upload to server
MISRA checkers are licensed separately — verify MISRA addon is included in your license
Project → Build → Issue hierarchy: filtering requires navigating this structure
Acquired by Perforce 2019 — contact Perforce for licensing, not original Klocwork channels

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced (self-hosted customer responsibility). ltoken file-based auth (less secure than OAuth2). RBAC via Klocwork Server. On-premise for air-gapped safety-critical environments. Safety-critical standards: DO-178C, ISO 26262, IEC 62443. Source code analyzed on-premise — sensitive IP stays in-house. Automotive/aerospace use cases require strict supply chain security.
